draft.title: a CRM for freelancers
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a CRM for freelancers

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers respond better to a friendly, approachable tone that feels like talking to a trusted peer rather than enterprise software
facade.label: Casual and warm
facade.acceptImplies: Lean into conversational copy, warm color palette, and personality in onboarding and empty states
facade.rejectImplies: Move toward polished, formal language and neutral enterprise aesthetic with minimal personality

evidence.hypothesis: Freelancers respond better to a friendly, approachable tone that feels like talking to a trusted peer rather than enterprise software
evidence.implication: Lean into conversational copy, warm color palette, and personality in onboarding and empty states

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer visual scanning of client info (cards) over dense database tables for quick relationship context
facade.label: Client list as cards
facade.acceptImplies: Interface should prioritize card-based layouts, thumbnails, and scannable chunks over tabular data for client management views
facade.rejectImplies: Tabular, spreadsheet-like layouts with full data rows may be more efficient; users want the fastest search/sort, not visual browsing

facade.hypothesis: Freelance CRM users prefer approachable, friendly interfaces over stark or corporate-feeling tools
facade.label: Soft rounded corners
facade.acceptImplies: Design leans toward curved buttons, gentle card borders, and organic shapes—feels collaborative and low-stress
facade.rejectImplies: Design should favor clean lines, rectangular grids, and sharp edges—feels professional and structured

facade.hypothesis: A CRM for freelancers works best with a calm, uncluttered interface that reduces cognitive load—showing only essential metrics and tasks without visual noise.
facade.label: Minimal dashboard
facade.acceptImplies: The interface should prioritize breathing room, soft typography, muted colors, and a zen-like workspace that feels meditative rather than stimulating.
facade.rejectImplies: The CRM should embrace visual density, bold call-to-actions, bright alerts, and an energetic dashboard packed with information and activity indicators that feel alive and urgent.

facade.hypothesis: Freelance CRM users strongly prefer static, instant-load contact displays over animated list transitions
facade.axisTargeted: Animation vs Instant — whether data surfaces should load/animate in or appear complete
facade.label: Instant client list
facade.acceptImplies: User values speed and clarity over visual flourish; will likely favor snappy interactions, instant feedback, minimal transitions
facade.rejectImplies: User enjoys motion as signal of responsiveness; may prefer animated reveals, staggered loads, or transition-based affordances that feel alive

facade.hypothesis: For a freelancer CRM, a persistent left sidebar navigation combined with tab-based content areas would balance quick access to client lists with organized project views within each section.
facade.axisTargeted: Navigation architecture: sidebar + tabs vs full-tab systems vs sidebar-only
facade.label: Sidebar with tabs inside
facade.acceptImplies: User values hybrid navigation that keeps context while allowing multi-view comparison (e.g., client list always visible while switching between projects, invoices, messages).
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers either purely flat tab navigation or single-column sidebar—either maximum simplicity or maximum focus, not layered hierarchy.

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers who value efficiency and credibility may prefer a professional, minimalist interface that signals competence and saves them time rather than charming them with personality
facade.label: All business, no nonsense
facade.acceptImplies: Lean into clean typography, neutral grays/blues, direct microcopy, zero decorative elements, dashboard-first design
facade.rejectImplies: Confirms that warmth and personality are non-negotiable—move further toward conversational tone, color, and character

axes.label: Whether they prefer minimal, clean aesthetics vs. rich visual hierarchy with color/imagery—reveals if they want a distraction-free workspace or one that feels more dynamic and branded
Whether they favor sidebar/drawer navigation vs. top tabs vs. floating action buttons—reveals their mental model for accessing client lists, invoices, and projects
Whether they want a professional/formal tone vs. casual/encouraging tone in copy and microcopy—reveals if they see the tool as business-critical infrastructure or a supportive assistant
Whether they prefer a calm, neutral workspace vs. one with energy and visual feedback—reveals if they want to feel grounded while managing chaos or energized by activity
Whether they want dense dashboards showing all metrics at once vs. progressive disclosure with drill-down—reveals how they process their own business data (big picture vs. detail-oriented)
Whether they prefer instant feedback (snappy, immediate) vs. smooth, animated transitions—reveals their tolerance for perceived speed and preference for reassuring motion vs. crisp responsiveness

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they prefer minimal, clean aesthetics vs. rich visual hierarchy with color/imagery—reveals if they want a distraction-free workspace or one that feels more dynamic and branded
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they favor sidebar/drawer navigation vs. top tabs vs. floating action buttons—reveals their mental model for accessing client lists, invoices, and projects
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they want a professional/formal tone vs. casual/encouraging tone in copy and microcopy—reveals if they see the tool as business-critical infrastructure or a supportive assistant
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they prefer a calm, neutral workspace vs. one with energy and visual feedback—reveals if they want to feel grounded while managing chaos or energized by activity
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they want dense dashboards showing all metrics at once vs. progressive disclosure with drill-down—reveals how they process their own business data (big picture vs. detail-oriented)
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they prefer instant feedback (snappy, immediate) vs. smooth, animated transitions—reveals their tolerance for perceived speed and preference for reassuring motion vs. crisp responsiveness
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo
